title: The magical S DAT
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] The magical S-DAT             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:15 am This is shaping up to be one of THE most important plot trinkets in Rebuild. First, a summary of 1.0 and 2.0: - first, as in NGE, escapist Shinji constantly plays tracks 25-26, the teenager stuck in a loop of unhapiness that he is. Yes, that was a Marble Hornets reference. - then Mari falls from the sky and knocks it off Shinji. Mari then hands it back to him, after possibly having tampered with it - after that it breaks the 25-26 loop and advances (see what I did there?) to track 27, which weirds Shinji out and symbolically marks the spot where Rebuild starts going completely off the rails - then, shit happens and Shinji decides to burn bridges, including dumping the S-DAT - it gets picked picked up by poka poka Rei - ...who takes it into the Zeruel battle and gets eaten along with it - when Shinji goes all RAGE and gets Rei back, her giant self appears to hold an equally oversized version of it - at this point, it's obviously not your regular S-DAT any more - along with Rei, it gets absorbed into God-Mode Unit 01 OK, now 3.0 - Shinji gets de-tanged, but apparently there's no trace of Rei in the plug. However, the S-DAT materializes. Now, here's my speculation: the Magical S-DAT currently holds Rei's soul. - The player then gets tampered with by [s]Jesus[/s]Kaworu. - (Before his death, Kaworu cryptically assures Shinji they can meet again - possibly he means another continuity meta-sequel theory style, but considering the tampering, could the S-DAT also hold part of Kaworu now?) [s]- in the end, the S-Dat, possibly holding Rei's soul , gets picked up by an apparently soulles Rei clone . See where this is heading? [/s] edit: apparently one of the reports I read was misleading - oh well, still waiting to see what happens to the player in 4.0.

[]:             Quote Post by os189 » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:19 am Kaworu/Jesus doesn't just "tamper" with it. He FIXES it. He brings it back from the dead. Before, it wasn't even turning on....just a blank grey screen on the LED. After Kaworu works his magic.....it plays again!! And at the end, it's discarded.....Rei Q does NOT pick it up to my best recollection. Where are people thinking they saw this?

[]:             Quote Post by Kozar927 » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:56 am According to many people Rei is not shown picking it up at any point. However that being said your theory is interesting. I think it may just be a symbol of his journey tho rather then a key plot point in any way

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Tue Nov 20, 2012 1:03 am Dang it, had to read a misleading report :/

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Nov 20, 2012 1:09 am I thought ReiRei (what was left of her) was stored in the Eva 01, but I kinda like your theory of her soul being in the SDAT. Q!Rei MIGHT have picked up the SDAT. We just did not see it happen (or not) on screen.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Nov 20, 2012 1:10 am LiLi wrote: ReiRei

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Tue Nov 20, 2012 1:28 am I predict that in 4.0, Shinji will realize that because it's now 2030, he should finally get around to upgrading and throw away the SDAT and order a used first generation iPod through the smouldering wreckage that was once eBay.

[]:             Quote Post by Grub » Tue Nov 20, 2012 2:23 am S-DAT probably just represents the Evangelion we all know. Movie's going off the rails, but Kaworu fixes it because, hey, we're back to the (general) story beats of Kaworu and Shinji from the original series! And then that all gets tossed aside too and, well, so much for Neon GenesiS-DATvangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Tue Nov 20, 2012 4:30 am ^I can see that as a favourable interpretation. In 2.0, the SDAT is shown to go over to 'Track 27'. In NGE, it had normally been seen to be playing 'Track 25' or 'Track 26', and so, viewers of the original saws saw 'Track 27' as an indication that the new films had - or were about to - go beyond the boundaries* of the original series. *or the 'frame' of the series, as the terms are more or less interchangeable. If the inclusion of the SDAT in 3.0 holds from its use as a literary device in 2.0, the choice of whether or not it is left behind at the ejected entry plug could be a metaphor: the staff are uncertain about continuing to use story ideas from NGE for FINAL. If the SDAT isn't included in FINAL, I could see others presenting that as a metaphor for the staff having abandoned any leftover inspirations from NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 20, 2012 4:44 am I don't know about the SDAT player holding the soul of Rei and Kaworu but I really hope that ReiQ recovered it off-screen or something, by the end of Ha the SDAT player stopped being a link to Gendo and it has become a link to other persons he loved, Rei and Kaworu, throwing it away would "symbolically" (I hate this word) represent that he's throwing away what he felt for them, it would be really sad. Well, it's not like I don't expect this and worse from Anno but still...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Nov 20, 2012 4:46 am This is the best theory of what happened to Rei ever. And symbolically connects Shinji's relationship with Rei to otaku escapism. Fuck yes.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Tue Nov 20, 2012 4:46 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: [the SDAT]t has become a link to other persons he loved, Rei and Kaworu, throwing it away would "symbolically" represent that he's throwing away what he felt for them

[]:             Quote Post by Coral » Tue Nov 20, 2012 4:46 am It's left ambiguous whether Rei Q picks it up, but honestly seeing her look at it makes it as good as said that she does. You wouldn't drag the damn thing through all three movies and then have Shinji merge with it if you didn't intend to actually use it for something, and abandoning it to symbolize the movie going off the rails is nonsensical, because it's already been used as a symbol of Rebuild's plot going off the rails. Multiple times. That said, I don't really think I'm a fan of Chekov's SDAT holding Poka-Rei's soul in it. Having Rei!Q hold it/listen to it and suddenly instantly regain her personality wraps things up a bit too neatly and in too saccharine a way for Rebuild. I feel it's just as likely that Rei's still in Unit 01 and NERV/WILL-E couldn't fully extract her because she's in too deep. After all, Yui Ayanami is still in there and Evangelion is a story that repeats.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:44 am Xard wrote: I like the Japanese fan name: Poka-Rei or Poka-Ayanami :)

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:50 am Well, did Q!Rei ever look meaningfully at the SDAT, and then there was a transition to another scene? Because that's how good Rei picked it up in 2.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Grub » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:19 pm EvangelionFan wrote: It's all the more interesting that, from what we have heard, the choice isn't up to Shinji: it is up to Q-Rei. From now, the SDAT is now about whether Q-Rei picks it up or not and what it means for her character if she does/doesn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:45 pm To the OP, not to sound offensive,but i call your theory BS. Rei wasnt giant inside the zeruel, just look at Shinji kun when he reaches her, the external reiruel form is not tha of "spirit" Rei that Shinji saves.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Tue Nov 20, 2012 2:09 pm Merkaba wrote: Like you, I'm calling BS. Why would Anno "forget about it?" It's his story, and it's a damn good one at that. Just like all of his works. I don't get why people keep saying that he and his staff are idiots or jumped the shark or are scrambling to fix their mistakes, etc. These people are masters of the medium, and I have faith that they're far more able to produce a worthwhile story than a bunch of nobodies (myself included) here on a fan forum.

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Tue Nov 20, 2012 2:13 pm Right, I need to rewatch 2.22. Still, the point stands: the S-DAT had to enter tangified state, migrate from Zeruel to Eva 01 along with Rei, and get de-tanged along with Shinji. That's quite a feat for a piece of audio equipment. And probably I wouldn't have thought of this theory if not for the specific way it gets reintroduced in Q. Basically, "No, we checked, Ayanami's not there, but this was, for some reason". To me it just openly invites this sort of speculation.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Nov 20, 2012 2:16 pm I'd assume Rei 2 is within Eva-01 (merged with Yui??), but her last words were "I'm sorry, I couldn't do anything" regarding that dinner party. Whether or not she and Yui merged together in Eva-01 during those 14 years, perhaps in Final we'll have Yui and Rei able to finally, properly, do something (whatever that would be) that helps Shinji and allows him to finally mature and be happy. A parallel to EoE at the very least. But yeah, I don't think the SDAT contains Rei 2's soul. It was found along with Shinji in the entry plug and Kaworu happened to fix it.
